category_title: Preschoolers who throw tantrums during clean-up transitions
minimal_context: Olivia is in the classroom dramatic play center and is asked to clean up her activities.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Olivia, it is time to clean up the dramatic play center.

STUDENT: No! I want to keep playing!

TUTOR: You need to put the toys away now.

STUDENT: (Starts crying loudly) I don't want to clean up!

TUTOR: Please start picking up the items.

STUDENT: (Drops to the floor sobbing and screaming)

options.A: Prompt, model, role-play, and explicitly teach desired social-emotional behaviors to the student during clean-up transitions.
options.B: Provide scheduled, non-contingent breaks during the dramatic play activities that are made available to the student for free.
options.C: Model complete sentences for the student as often as possible during the dramatic play activities.
options.D: Review a Thumbs Up sheet, having the student count the circled thumbs up and thumbs down to discuss her overall day.
